# komodo_repo (Resource)

Manages a Komodo git repository resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "komodo_repo" "example" {
  name = "my-repo"

  source = {
    url    = "https://github.com"
    path   = "owner/my-repo"
    branch = "main"
  }
}
```

### With webhook and on_clone hook

```terraform
resource "komodo_repo" "with_webhook" {
  name = "my-repo-with-webhook"

  provider = {
    path   = "owner/my-repo"
    branch = "main"
  }

  webhook = {
    enabled = true
  }

  on_clone = {
    path    = "/opt/app"
    command = "make install"
  }
}
```

### With environment variables

```terraform
resource "komodo_repo" "with_env" {
  name = "my-repo-with-env"

  environment = {
    file_path = ".env"
    variables = {
      APP_ENV = "production"
      PORT    = "8080"
    }
  }
}
```

## Argument Reference

- `name` (Required, Forces New) - The unique name of the git repository.
- `server_id` (Optional) - The ID of the server to clone the repo on. Omit or set to empty string to disconnect.
- `builder_id` (Optional) - The ID of the builder to attach. Omit or set to empty string to disconnect.
- `path` (Optional) - The folder on the server to clone into. Omit or set to empty string to clear.
- `links` (Optional, Computed) - Quick links associated with this repository.
- `source` (Optional) - Git provider configuration. See [source](#nestedatt--source) below.
- `webhook` (Optional, Computed) - Webhook configuration. See [webhook](#nestedatt--webhook) below.
- `on_clone` (Optional) - A command to run after the repository is cloned. See [on_clone / on_pull](#nestedatt--on_clone) below.
- `on_pull` (Optional) - A command to run after the repository is pulled. See [on_clone / on_pull](#nestedatt--on_pull) below.
- `environment` (Optional) - Environment variable configuration. See [environment](#nestedatt--environment) below.

<a id="nestedatt--source"></a>
### Nested Schema for `source`

- `url` (Optional) - The URL of the git provider, e.g. `https://github.com`.
- `account_id` (Optional) - The git account to use for private repositories. Omit or set to empty string to disconnect.
- `path` (Optional) - The repository to clone, e.g. `owner/repo`. Omit or set to empty string to clear.
- `branch` (Optional) - The branch to check out. Omit or set to empty string to clear.
- `commit` (Optional) - A specific commit hash to check out. Omit or set to empty string to clear.

<a id="nestedatt--webhook"></a>
### Nested Schema for `webhook`

- `enabled` (Optional, Computed) - Whether webhooks should trigger an action on this repository.
- `secret` (Optional) - An alternate webhook secret for this repository.

<a id="nestedatt--on_clone"></a>
<a id="nestedatt--on_pull"></a>
### Nested Schema for `on_clone` / `on_pull`

- `path` (Optional, Computed) - The working directory for the command.
- `command` (Optional, Computed) - The shell command to run.

<a id="nestedatt--environment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `environment`

- `file_path` (Optional) - Path to the environment file. Defaults to `.env`.
- `variables` (Optional) - Map of environment variables to inject. Keys are automatically uppercased.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to all arguments above, the following attributes are exported:

- `id` - The git repository identifier (ObjectId).

## Import

Git repositories can be imported using their ObjectId:

```shell
terraform import komodo_repo.example <id>
```
